papers.title: ChronosAudio: A Comprehensive Long-Audio Benchmark for Evaluating Audio-Large Language Models
papers.titleJa: ChronosAudio: 音響大規模言語モデルの評価のための包括的な長時間音声ベンチマーク
papers.what: ① 音響大規模言語モデル（ALLMs）の長時間音響理解能力を評価するための初のマルチタスクベンチマークであるChronosAudioを提案。
papers.novel: ② 長時間音響理解の課題に特化した初のベンチマークを構築し、既存モデルの性能限界と課題を明らかにした点。
papers.method: ③ 短時間、中時間、長時間のカテゴリに分けた36,000のテストインスタンスを含むデータセットを用い、16の最先端モデルを評価。
papers.validation: ④ ChronosAudioを用いて、ALLMsが長時間音声に対して90%以上の性能低下を示すことを実験的に確認。
papers.discussion: ⑤ 長時間音声における性能低下の原因として、注意メカニズムの拡散や時間的局所性の維持の失敗を指摘。現行の緩和策では50%の回復にとどまる。

nextReads.label: AudioLM: a Language Modeling Approach to Audio Generation (2022)

papers.abstract: Although Audio Large Language Models (ALLMs) have witnessed substantial advancements, their long audio understanding capabilities remain unexplored. A plethora of benchmarks have been proposed for general audio tasks, they predominantly focus on short-form clips, leaving without a consensus on evaluating ALLMs over extended durations. This paper proposes ChronosAudio, the first multi-task benchmark tailored for long-audio understanding in ALLMs. It encompasses six major task categories and comprises 36,000 test instances totaling over 200 hours audio, stratified into short, middle, and long-form categories to comprehensively evaluate length generalization. Extensive experiments on 16 state-of-the-art models using ChronosAudio yield three critical findings: 1.Precipitous Long-Context Collapse: ALLMs exhibit a severe inability to sustain performance, with the transition from short to long contexts triggering a staggering performance degradation of over 90% in specific tasks. 2.Structural Attention Dilution: Performance degradation stems from a fundamental failure in maintaining temporal locality; attention mechanisms suffer from significant diffusion in later sequences. 3.Restorative Ceiling of Mitigation: Current strategies only offer 50% recovery. These findings reveal significant challenges in long-audio, underscoring the urgent need for approaches to achieve robust, document-level audio reasoning.

papers.title: When Tone and Words Disagree: Towards Robust Speech Emotion Recognition under Acoustic-Semantic Conflict
papers.titleJa: 音調と単語が矛盾するとき: 音響-意味の対立下での頑健な音声感情認識を目指して
papers.what: ① 音声感情認識（SER）モデルが音響と意味の対立に直面した際の性能低下を解決するため、新しいフレームワークFASを提案。
papers.novel: ② 音響と意味の経路を明示的に分離し、軽量なクエリベースの注意モジュールで結合する新しいアプローチを提案。
papers.method: ③ 音響と意味を分離するFusion Acoustic-Semantic (FAS) フレームワークを開発し、CASEデータセットで評価。
papers.validation: ④ CASEベンチマークで従来のSERモデルを大幅に上回り、59.38%の精度で新たなSOTAを達成。
papers.discussion: ⑤ 音響-意味の対立におけるモデルの性能限界を指摘し、さらなる改善の余地を示唆。

nextReads.label: Speech Emotion Recognition: Features and Models (2021)
Self-Supervised Learning for Speech Emotion Recognition (2022)

papers.abstract: Speech Emotion Recognition (SER) systems often assume congruence between vocal emotion and lexical semantics. However, in real-world interactions, acoustic-semantic conflict is common yet overlooked, where the emotion conveyed by tone contradicts the literal meaning of spoken words. We show that state-of-the-art SER models, including ASR-based, self-supervised learning (SSL) approaches and Audio Language Models (ALMs), suffer performance degradation under such conflicts due to semantic bias or entangled acoustic-semantic representations. To address this, we propose the Fusion Acoustic-Semantic (FAS) framework, which explicitly disentangles acoustic and semantic pathways and bridges them through a lightweight, query-based attention module. To enable systematic evaluation, we introduce the Conflict in Acoustic-Semantic Emotion (CASE), the first dataset dominated by clear and interpretable acoustic-semantic conflicts in varied scenarios. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FAS consistently outperforms existing methods in both in-domain and zero-shot settings. Notably, on the CASE benchmark, conventional SER models fail dramatically, while FAS sets a new SOTA with 59.38% accuracy. Our code and datasets is available at https://github.com/24DavidHuang/FAS.

papers.title: Analyzing Reasoning Shifts in Audio Deepfake Detection under Adversarial Attacks: The Reasoning Tax versus Shield Bifurcation
papers.titleJa: 音響ディープフェイク検出における推論の変化を分析: 推論の負担と防御の分岐
papers.what: ① 音響ディープフェイク検出（ADD）における推論のロバスト性を、敵対的攻撃下での変化を通じて評価するフレームワークを提案。
papers.novel: ② 推論のロバスト性を音響認識、認知的一貫性、認知的不協和の3次元で評価する新しい法医学的監査フレームワークを提案。
papers.method: ③ 敵対的攻撃に対するALMsの推論の変化を分析し、推論が防御として機能する場合と性能低下を引き起こす場合の分岐を特定。
papers.validation: ④ 敵対的攻撃下でのALMsの推論シフトを体系的に分析し、分類が失敗しても高い認知的不協和が潜在的な操作を示す可能性を示唆。
papers.discussion: ⑤ 推論が必ずしもロバスト性を向上させない場合があることを指摘し、特に言語的攻撃下での認知的一貫性の低下が課題。

nextReads.label: Explainable AI for Audio Deepfake Detection (2022)

papers.abstract: Audio Language Models (ALMs) offer a promising shift towards explainable audio deepfake detections (ADDs), moving beyond \textit{black-box} classifiers by providing some level of transparency into their predictions via reasoning traces. This necessitates a new class of model robustness analysis: robustness of the predictive reasoning under adversarial attacks, which goes beyond existing paradigm that mainly focuses on the shifts of the final predictions (e.g., fake v.s. real). To analyze such reasoning shifts, we introduce a forensic auditing framework to evaluate the robustness of ALMs' reasoning under adversarial attacks in three inter-connected dimensions: acoustic perception, cognitive coherence, and cognitive dissonance. Our systematic analysis reveals that explicit reasoning does not universally enhance robustness. Instead, we observe a bifurcation: for models exhibiting robust acoustic perception, reasoning acts as a defensive \textit{``shield''}, protecting them from adversarial attacks. However, for others, it imposes a performance \textit{``tax''}, particularly under linguistic attacks which reduce cognitive coherence and increase attack success rate. Crucially, even when classification fails, high cognitive dissonance can serve as a \textit{silent alarm}, flagging potential manipulation. Overall, this work provides a critical evaluation of the role of reasoning in forensic audio deepfake analysis and its vulnerabilities.

papers.title: The Sonar Moment: Benchmarking Audio-Language Models in Audio Geo-Localization
papers.titleJa: ソナーモーメント: 音声言語モデルにおける音声地理的ローカライズのベンチマーク
papers.what: ① 音声言語モデル（ALMs）の地理的ローカライズ能力を評価するための初のベンチマークAGL1Kを提案。
papers.novel: ② 72カ国・地域をカバーする1,444の高品質な音声と位置情報のペアを含むデータセットを構築し、音声地理的ローカライズの研究を促進。
papers.method: ③ Audio Localizabilityという新しい指標を提案し、信頼性の高いローカライズ可能な音声サンプルを選別。
papers.validation: ④ 16のALMsを評価し、クローズドソースモデルがオープンソースモデルを大幅に上回ることを実証。
papers.discussion: ⑤ 言語的手がかりが予測の主な支えとなっている点や、地域的バイアスやエラー原因を分析し、さらなる改善の方向性を示唆。
papers.abstract: Geo-localization aims to infer the geographic origin of a given signal. In computer vision, geo-localization has served as a demanding benchmark for compositional reasoning and is relevant to public safety. In contrast, progress on audio geo-localization has been constrained by the lack of high-quality audio-location pairs. To address this gap, we introduce AGL1K, the first audio geo-localization benchmark for audio language models (ALMs), spanning 72 countries and territories. To extract reliably localizable samples from a crowd-sourced platform, we propose the Audio Localizability metric that quantifies the informativeness of each recording, yielding 1,444 curated audio clips. Evaluations on 16 ALMs show that ALMs have emerged with audio geo-localization capability. We find that closed-source models substantially outperform open-source models, and that linguistic clues often dominate as a scaffold for prediction. We further analyze ALMs' reasoning traces, regional bias, error causes, and the interpretability of the localizability metric. Overall, AGL1K establishes a benchmark for audio geo-localization and may advance ALMs with better geospatial reasoning capability.

papers.title: Interpretable All-Type Audio Deepfake Detection with Audio LLMs via Frequency-Time Reinforcement Learning
papers.titleJa: 音響LLMを用いた周波数-時間強化学習による解釈可能な全タイプ音響ディープフェイク検出
papers.what: 音響大規模言語モデル（ALLMs）を活用して、音声、環境音、歌声、音楽など多様な音声タイプに対応するディープフェイク検出モデルを提案しています。
papers.novel: 従来の二値分類に基づく手法のブラックボックス性や、強化学習の報酬ハッキング問題を克服するため、周波数-時間構造化チェインオブソート（CoT）を用いた新しい学習パラダイムを導入しています。
papers.method: Cold-startデータセットを用いた教師あり微調整（SFT）と、ルールベースの周波数-時間制約を組み込んだ相対ポリシー最適化（FT-GRPO）を2段階で適用する手法を提案しています。
papers.validation: 提案手法は、全タイプの音響ディープフェイク検出タスクにおいて最先端の性能を達成し、解釈可能な理由付けを提供することを実証しました。
papers.discussion: 現実世界の複雑な音声データに対する一般化性能や、さらなる解釈可能性の向上が今後の課題として挙げられます。

nextReads.label: Audio Deepfake Detection with Self-supervised Learning (2023)

papers.abstract: Recent advances in audio large language models (ALLMs) have made high-quality synthetic audio widely accessible, increasing the risk of malicious audio deepfakes across speech, environmental sounds, singing voice, and music. Real-world audio deepfake detection (ADD) therefore requires all-type detectors that generalize across heterogeneous audio and provide interpretable decisions. Given the strong multi-task generalization ability of ALLMs, we first investigate their performance on all-type ADD under both supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement fine-tuning (RFT). However, SFT using only binary real/fake labels tends to reduce the model to a black-box classifier, sacrificing interpretability. Meanwhile, vanilla RFT under sparse supervision is prone to reward hacking and can produce hallucinated, ungrounded rationales. To address this, we propose an automatic annotation and polishing pipeline that constructs Frequency-Time structured chain-of-thought (CoT) rationales, producing ~340K cold-start demonstrations. Building on CoT data, we propose Frequency Time-Group Relative Policy Optimization (FT-GRPO), a two-stage training paradigm that cold-starts ALLMs with SFT and then applies GRPO under rule-based frequency-time constraints. Experiments demonstrate that FT-GRPO achieves state-of-the-art performance on all-type ADD while producing interpretable, FT-grounded rationales. The data and code are available online.

papers.title: MoE Adapter for Large Audio Language Models: Sparsity, Disentanglement, and Gradient-Conflict-Free
papers.titleJa: 大規模音声言語モデルのためのMoEアダプター: スパース性、分離性、勾配競合の解消
papers.what: 音声情報の多様な属性（音声、音楽、環境音など）を分離して処理するためのスパースな専門家混合（MoE）アーキテクチャを提案しています。
papers.novel: 既存の密結合型アダプターの勾配競合問題を解消し、音響トークンを専門家に動的にルーティングすることで、より細かい特徴学習を可能にしています。
papers.method: 動的ゲーティング機構を用いて音響トークンを専門家に割り当てるMoEアーキテクチャを採用し、グローバルコンテキストを保持しつつ、補完的な特徴空間をキャプチャします。
papers.validation: 音声セマンティックタスクと副次的言語タスクの両方で、密結合型ベースラインを上回る性能を示し、計算コストは同等であることを確認しました。
papers.discussion: さらなる研究として、異なる音声属性間の相互作用をより深く理解するための分析が必要です。

nextReads.label: Sparse Neural Networks for Multimodal Learning (2022)

papers.abstract: Extending the input modality of Large Language Models~(LLMs) to the audio domain is essential for achieving comprehensive multimodal perception. However, it is well-known that acoustic information is intrinsically \textit{heterogeneous}, entangling attributes such as speech, music, and environmental context. Existing research is limited to a dense, parameter-shared adapter to model these diverse patterns, which induces \textit{gradient conflict} during optimization, as parameter updates required for distinct attributes contradict each other. To address this limitation, we introduce the \textit{\textbf{MoE-Adapter}}, a sparse Mixture-of-Experts~(MoE) architecture designed to decouple acoustic information. Specifically, it employs a dynamic gating mechanism that routes audio tokens to specialized experts capturing complementary feature subspaces while retaining shared experts for global context, thereby mitigating gradient conflicts and enabling fine-grained feature learning. Comprehensive experiments show that the MoE-Adapter achieves superior performance on both audio semantic and paralinguistic tasks, consistently outperforming dense linear baselines with comparable computational costs. Furthermore, we will release the related code and models to facilitate future research.

papers.title: The World is Not Mono: Enabling Spatial Understanding in Large Audio-Language Models
papers.titleJa: 世界はモノラルではない: 大規模音声言語モデルにおける空間的理解の実現
papers.what: 大規模音声言語モデル（LALMs）に空間的理解能力を付与し、音声シーン解析（ASA）のための新しい階層的フレームワークを提案しています。
papers.novel: 音声シーン解析における空間的次元の理解を可能にするため、スケーラブルなシミュレーションパイプラインや統一モデルフレームワーク、進化的トレーニングカリキュラムを導入しています。
papers.method: 高品質なアンビソニックデータを生成するシミュレーションパイプラインと、空間エンコーディングを統合したハイブリッド投影メカニズムを用いて、空間的知性を強化します。
papers.validation: 音声シーン解析のための包括的なベンチマークを開発し、空間的理解能力において提案モデルが優れた性能を示すことを実証しました。
papers.discussion: 空間的理解をさらに向上させるため、より多様な音響環境や複雑なシナリオでの評価が必要です。
papers.abstract: Existing large audio-language models perceive the world as "mono"-a single stream of audio that ignores the critical spatial dimension ("where") required for universal audio scene analysis (ASA). To bridge this gap, we first introduce a hierarchical framework for audio scene analysis. Guided by this framework, we introduce a system that enables large audio-language models (LALMs) to understand and reason about the complex acoustic world.   Our system endows LALMs with universal spatial understanding through four key innovations: (1) A scalable simulation pipeline that synthesizes high-quality First-Order-Ambisonics(FOA) data; (2) A unified model framework that integrates universal spatial encoding with a dense hybrid projection mechanism to bridge the modality gap; (3) A progressive training curriculum that evolves from representation alignment to reinforcement learning-based reasoning; and (4) A comprehensive benchmark for audio scene analysis (ASA) designed to rigorously evaluate atomic perception, relational integration, and cognitive reasoning capabilities, on which our model demonstrates comparatively strong capability for spatial understanding. Our work provides a clear pathway for leveraging the powerful reasoning abilities of LALMs towards holistic ASA, advancing from "mono" semantic recognition to spatial intelligence.

papers.title: UltraEval-Audio: A Unified Framework for Comprehensive Evaluation of Audio Foundation Models
papers.titleJa: UltraEval-Audio: 音響基盤モデルの包括的評価のための統一フレームワーク
papers.what: 音響基盤モデルの理解および生成タスクを包括的に評価するための統一フレームワークであるUltraEval-Audioを提案しています。
papers.novel: 音声評価の統一されたフレームワークを提供し、特に音響コーデックの評価において、意味的正確性、音色の忠実性、音響品質の3つの次元での新しい評価スキームを導入しています。
papers.method: モジュラーアーキテクチャを採用し、10言語と14のタスクカテゴリをサポートする評価フレームワークを構築し、リアルタイムの公開リーダーボードを提供します。
papers.validation: 24の主流モデルと36の権威あるベンチマークを統合し、提案フレームワークの有効性を実証しました。
papers.discussion: 中国語以外の多言語における評価基準のさらなる拡充や、モデルの多様性に対応した評価の深化が課題です。
papers.abstract: The development of audio foundation models has accelerated rapidly since the emergence of GPT-4o. However, the lack of comprehensive evaluation has become a critical bottleneck for further progress in the field, particularly in audio generation. Current audio evaluation faces three major challenges: (1) audio evaluation lacks a unified framework, with datasets and code scattered across various sources, hindering fair and efficient cross-model comparison;(2) audio codecs, as a key component of audio foundation models, lack a widely accepted and holistic evaluation methodology; (3) existing speech benchmarks are heavily reliant on English, making it challenging to objectively assess models' performance on Chinese. To address the first issue, we introduce UltraEval-Audio, a unified evaluation framework for audio foundation models, specifically designed for both audio understanding and generation tasks. UltraEval-Audio features a modular architecture, supporting 10 languages and 14 core task categories, while seamlessly integrating 24 mainstream models and 36 authoritative benchmarks. To enhance research efficiency, the framework provides a one-command evaluation feature, accompanied by real-time public leaderboards. For the second challenge, UltraEval-Audio adopts a novel comprehensive evaluation scheme for audio codecs, evaluating performance across three key dimensions: semantic accuracy, timbre fidelity, and acoustic quality. To address the third issue, we propose two new Chinese benchmarks, SpeechCMMLU and SpeechHSK, designed to assess Chinese knowledge proficiency and language fluency. We wish that UltraEval-Audio will provide both academia and industry with a transparent, efficient, and fair platform for comparison of audio models. Our code, benchmarks, and leaderboards are available at https://github.com/OpenBMB/UltraEval-Audio.

papers.title: Sound Event Detection with Boundary-Aware Optimization and Inference
papers.titleJa: 境界認識最適化と推論を用いた音響イベント検出
papers.what: ① 音響イベント検出（SED）における時間的イベントの検出精度を向上させるため、境界認識最適化と推論戦略を提案。
papers.novel: ② イベントの開始点と終了点を明示的にモデル化し、従来のフレーム単位のアプローチを超える性能を実現。
papers.method: ③ Recurrent Event Detection（RED）とEvent Proposal Network（EPN）の新しい時間モデリング層を導入し、特化した損失関数を使用。
papers.validation: ④ AudioSetの時間的に強くアノテーションされたデータセットを用いて評価し、従来のフレーム単位モデルを上回る性能を実証。
papers.discussion: ⑤ ポストプロセッシングのハイパーパラメータ調整を不要にしたが、他のデータセットでの一般化性能についてはさらなる検証が必要。

nextReads.label: AudioSet: An ontology and human-labeled dataset for audio events (2017)
Sound Event Detection in Multichannel Audio (2020)

papers.abstract: Temporal detection problems appear in many fields including time-series estimation, activity recognition and sound event detection (SED). In this work, we propose a new approach to temporal event modeling by explicitly modeling event onsets and offsets, and by introducing boundary-aware optimization and inference strategies that substantially enhance temporal event detection. The presented methodology incorporates new temporal modeling layers - Recurrent Event Detection (RED) and Event Proposal Network (EPN) - which, together with tailored loss functions, enable more effective and precise temporal event detection. We evaluate the proposed method in the SED domain using a subset of the temporally-strongly annotated portion of AudioSet. Experimental results show that our approach not only outperforms traditional frame-wise SED models with state-of-the-art post-processing, but also removes the need for post-processing hyperparameter tuning, and scales to achieve new state-of-the-art performance across all AudioSet Strong classes.
papers.comment: Submitted to IEEE Signal Processing Letters

trend: ① 音声大規模言語モデル（ALLMs）の性能評価と応用が進展し、長時間音声理解（ChronosAudio）や地理的ローカライズ（AGL1K）、包括的評価（UltraEval-Audio）に焦点が当てられている。
② 音響イベント検出（SED）や音声感情認識（SER）において、境界認識最適化や音響・意味の対立を考慮した新しい手法が提案され、精度とロバスト性の向上が図られている。
③ 音声ディープフェイク検出（ADD）や空間的理解能力の強化に向け、敵対的攻撃への耐性や音声シーン解析を支援する新たなフレームワークが開発されている。
